crime.value: Murder of Gorgiano, an Italian criminal who was tracking down victims
crime.quote: he knew Gorgiano to be close upon him, and that, thank God! he was ready for him when he came

victim.value: Gorgiano, a dangerous Italian criminal with 'dreadful powers' who was hunting his targets
victim.quote: Both Italy and America are full of stories of his dreadful powers

closed_world.value: The London boarding house and surrounding Howe Street area where the Italian couple was hiding
closed_world.quote: I saw two Italians watching the house, and I understood that in some way Gorgiano had found our retreat

culprit_and_motive.value: Gennaro killed Gorgiano in self-defense to protect himself and his wife from the pursuing criminal
culprit_and_motive.quote: I would ask you whether we have anything to fear from the law...for what he has done?

resolution.value: Holmes deduces the truth through observation and the wife's confession; authorities likely to exonerate due to self-defense
resolution.quote: If what she says is corroborated, I do not think she or her husband has much to fear

investigator.value: Sherlock Holmes, consulting detective called in by the landlady Mrs. Warren about her mysterious lodger
investigator.quote: So spoke Sherlock Holmes and turned back to the great scrapbook

suspect_architecture.value: Limited suspect pool: Gennaro (the mysterious lodger), Gorgiano (the pursuer), with Mrs. Warren as concerned observer. Motive centers on Italian criminal vendetta and protection of wife.
suspect_architecture.quote: Both Italy and America are full of stories of his dreadful powers. If ever they were exerted it would be now.

red_herring.value: The mysterious behavior of the lodger appears criminal but is actually protective - hiding to shield his wife from danger rather than concealing guilt.
red_herring.quote: What is he hiding for? What has he done? Except for the girl, I am all alone in the house with him

clue_fairness.value: Italian light signals decoded in real-time before reader: 'ATTENTA' (beware) and 'PERICOLO' (danger), with Holmes explaining the translation as he discovers it.
clue_fairness.quote: it is Italian! The A means that it is addressed to a woman. 'Beware! Beware! Beware!' How's that, Watson?

social_world.value: Cross-Atlantic criminal networks spanning Italy and America, with law enforcement cooperation between British, American, and Italian police.
social_world.quote: he wished to be free that he might communicate both with the American and with the Italian police

reveal_mechanic.value: Light signal decryption demonstrates the protective rather than criminal nature of the mysterious behavior, revealing the true threat comes from the pursuer.
reveal_mechanic.quote: Finally Gennaro told me, through the paper, that he would signal to me from a certain window

media_and_audience.value: Newspaper used as covert communication channel between separated spouses, with public perception shaped by fear of foreign criminal elements.
media_and_audience.quote: All that I learned was through the columns of a newspaper

investigator_wound.value: Holmes initially dismisses the case as unworthy of his time, but responds to flattery and appeals to his kindness, suggesting vulnerability to emotional manipulation.
investigator_wound.quote: Holmes was accessible upon the side of flattery, and also, to do him justice, upon the side of kindliness

_meta.title: ebook sir arthur conan doyle the adventure of the red circle
